title: These stocks reporting next week are seeing their earnings momentum rise
summary:
Next week marks a significant period for the first-quarter earnings season, with 88 companies in the S&P 500 set to report their results, representing over 17% of the benchmark index. Among these, notable names include Vertiv and Amazon, both of which are experiencing strong earnings momentum. Analysts have recently become more optimistic about these stocks, with many revising their earnings estimates upward. This trend indicates a growing confidence in the financial performance of these companies as they prepare to disclose their latest results.

Vertiv, a provider of infrastructure and data center services, is particularly noteworthy, having seen its shares surge by 89% this year. Bank of America has reaffirmed its buy rating on Vertiv, raising its price target from $277 to $330, which suggests an additional 12% upside potential. The bank's analyst, Andrew Obin, cites improved industry trends and anticipates that Vertiv will deliver results that exceed expectations, supported by a significant increase in capital expenditures from major cloud service providers projected to reach $715 billion in 2026.

Amazon, another key player reporting next week, has also seen its stock rise by 10% this year. As the leading e-commerce platform in the U.S., Amazon's earnings report is highly anticipated, and analysts expect it could benefit from a post-earnings boost. The overall sentiment heading into next week’s earnings reports is bullish, with many analysts highlighting the potential for upward revisions in earnings estimates and positive price targets across various sectors, reflecting a robust outlook for the market.
social_text: Next week, 88 S&P 500 companies report earnings, including Vertiv, which has surged 89% this year. Bank of America raised its price target to $330, citing strong industry trends. Amazon also set to report, up 10% YTD.

title: Tim Cook stepping down as Apple CEO, John Ternus taking over
summary:
In a significant leadership change, Tim Cook, who has been at the helm of Apple since 2011, will step down as CEO on September 1, 2023. Cook succeeded the late Steve Jobs and has played a crucial role in steering Apple through a transformative decade, overseeing the launch of groundbreaking products and services that have solidified Apple's position as a leader in the tech industry. His tenure has been marked by a focus on innovation, sustainability, and expanding Apple's ecosystem, which has significantly increased the company's market capitalization.

John Ternus, currently the Senior Vice President of Hardware Engineering, will take over as CEO. Ternus has been with Apple for 25 years and has been instrumental in the development of key hardware products, including the iPhone and Mac. Cook expressed his full confidence in Ternus's abilities, describing him as a visionary leader with a strong engineering background and a commitment to integrity. This transition marks a pivotal moment for Apple as it prepares to navigate the future under Ternus's leadership.

As Cook transitions to the role of Executive Chairman, he will continue to influence Apple's strategic direction. This leadership change is expected to be closely watched by investors and industry analysts, as it could signal new priorities and innovations for the tech giant. With Ternus at the helm, Apple aims to maintain its competitive edge and continue its legacy of innovation in an ever-evolving market landscape.
social_text: Tim Cook will step down as Apple CEO on September 1, 2023, after 12 years of leadership. John Ternus, SVP of Hardware Engineering, will succeed him, bringing 25 years of experience to the role. Cook will become Executive Chairman.

title: Profit Jumped 58% at Taiwan Semi. Does That Make TSM Stock a Buy Here?
summary:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (TSM) has reported a remarkable 58% year-over-year increase in net profit for the first quarter, significantly surpassing market expectations. This surge is attributed to the growing demand for advanced semiconductors, particularly the cutting-edge 3nm nodes that are essential for artificial intelligence (AI) applications. Major clients, including hyperscalers and AI innovators, are securing production capacity well in advance, which not only strengthens TSM's pricing power but also enhances its long-term revenue visibility. The company's performance underscores its pivotal role in the ongoing AI boom, positioning it as a key player in the semiconductor industry.

Management has expressed confidence in sustaining this momentum, raising its revenue growth outlook for 2026 to over 30%. To meet the escalating demand, TSMC plans to increase its capital expenditures significantly, aiming to expand its manufacturing capacity. This strategic move is designed to capture the next wave of AI-driven demand, indicating that the company is preparing for a future where advanced chips will be even more critical. However, despite these positive indicators, the stock market's reaction has been somewhat tepid, reflecting investor concerns about potential capacity constraints and the implications of heavy capital spending on profitability.

Founded in 1987, TSMC has established itself as the world's leading dedicated semiconductor foundry, with a market capitalization of approximately $1.92 trillion. The company operates multiple fabrication plants across Taiwan and internationally, including facilities in the U.S. and Europe. While TSMC's stock has performed well over the past year, recent fluctuations highlight the complexities of navigating investor sentiment amidst rapid growth and significant capital investment in the semiconductor sector.
social_text: TSMC's net profit soared 58% YoY, driven by demand for 3nm chips in AI. The company raised its 2026 revenue growth outlook to over 30%, but stock dipped amid investor concerns over capacity and spending.

title: This Trash‑Hauling Giant Could Be the Only Industrial Stock I'd Buy and Never Sell
summary:
WM (Waste Management, Inc.) stands out as a resilient player in the industrial sector, particularly in the waste management industry, which is often less susceptible to economic downturns compared to other industrial stocks. With a robust infrastructure that includes North America's largest disposal network and collection fleet, WM has established itself as a leader in providing comprehensive environmental solutions. The company operates a vertically integrated asset network comprising 506 waste transfer facilities, 262 landfills, 105 recycling facilities, and 18 medical waste incinerators, positioning it as the largest entity in the $125 billion North American waste and recycling market, generating $22.1 billion in revenue. This scale not only enhances operational efficiency but also allows WM to maintain a competitive edge in the industry.

WM's financial performance reflects its ability to navigate economic cycles effectively. The company set ambitious financial targets for 2019-2021, aiming for 4%-6% organic compound annual revenue growth and 5%-7% growth in adjusted operating earnings and free cash flow. Despite the challenges posed by the pandemic, WM managed to achieve a 3% annualized revenue growth rate while delivering impressive earnings growth of 7% and a remarkable 14% increase in free cash flow during the same period. The company's strong operational metrics have continued to improve, with projections indicating even better results for the 2022-2024 period, showcasing its resilience and adaptability in a fluctuating economic landscape.

As the largest recycler and a leader in renewable natural gas production, WM is not only focused on profitability but also on sustainability. Its commitment to environmental stewardship and innovation in waste management practices positions it favorably for long-term growth. Investors looking for a stable industrial stock may find WM to be an attractive option due to its consistent performance, strategic market positioning, and the essential nature of its services, which are likely to remain in demand regardless of economic conditions.
social_text: WM leads the $125B North American waste sector with $22.1B in revenue, operating 506 transfer facilities and 262 landfills. It targets 4%-6% revenue growth, showcasing resilience even during economic downturns.

title: The AI trade is fueling the market to record highs despite Iran war
summary:
The ongoing AI trade has propelled the stock market to unprecedented heights, even amidst the geopolitical tensions stemming from the Iran war. Despite fears of potential supply shocks affecting essential commodities like natural gas and helium, the semiconductor and tech sectors have shown remarkable resilience. The iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX) has experienced a significant surge of over 30% since its low on March 30, driven by the strong performance of major players in the industry, including Nvidia, Amazon, Alphabet, Meta, and Tesla, all of which have seen double-digit percentage increases. This bullish trend in the tech sector has also positively impacted the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV), which has rebounded from previous lows.

Oil prices have remained unstable due to the ongoing conflict, yet the high margins within the tech sector provide a buffer against rising commodity costs associated with chip and server production. This situation is particularly advantageous for Asian economies that depend on energy imports from the Middle East. Chetan Ahya, chief Asia economist at Morgan Stanley, highlighted that companies reliant on helium and sulfur are still able to afford higher prices, indicating a robust demand for these materials. This dynamic is crucial for economies like Korea and Taiwan, which are benefiting from the tech sector's profitability.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) has also reported that it is well-prepared to handle any near-term operational impacts due to its diversified supply chain for specialty chemicals and gases. CFO Wendell Huang emphasized the company's proactive measures, including maintaining safety stock inventories and collaborating with local energy providers to ensure a stable energy supply. As the tech sector continues to thrive, it remains to be seen how geopolitical factors will influence market dynamics in the coming months, but the current outlook remains optimistic for the industry.
social_text: The AI trade has driven the market to record highs, with the iShares Semiconductor ETF up over 30% since March 30. Major players like Nvidia and Amazon see double-digit gains, showcasing tech sector resilience amid geopolitical tensions.

title: Analysis: Investors pile into US stocks as 'TINA' revival knocks 'TIARA' trades
summary:
The recent U.S.-Iran ceasefire has significantly impacted investor sentiment, leading to a resurgence of TINA (There Is No Alternative) trades in the U.S. stock market. Following the ceasefire announcement on April 7, Wall Street has seen a remarkable recovery, with global investors injecting a net $28 billion into U.S. equities. This influx comes after a challenging period where U.S. stocks experienced a net outflow of $56 billion, primarily due to geopolitical tensions and rising energy prices. The renewed confidence in the U.S. economy is bolstered by strong earnings growth and the country's relative insulation from energy shocks, making it an attractive destination for investors once again.

The ceasefire has not only restored investor confidence but also highlighted the resilience of the U.S. market compared to its global counterparts. As earnings season unfolds, early indicators suggest that the U.S. economy remains robust, with the S&P 500 trading 2% above pre-war levels. This performance contrasts sharply with the broader market, which has only recently begun to recover from the losses incurred during the conflict. Analysts, including Michael Browne from the Franklin Templeton Institute, emphasize that the U.S. continues to outperform due to its long-term investment strategies and strong economic fundamentals.

The shift back to TINA trades reflects a broader trend where investors are increasingly favoring U.S. equities over international markets. The combination of a stable economic outlook, government spending, and the ongoing AI boom has made U.S. stocks particularly appealing. As the geopolitical landscape evolves, the focus remains on which markets will sustain their growth, with the U.S. currently leading the charge. This renewed interest in U.S. equities signals a potential shift in investment strategies as global investors reassess their portfolios in light of recent developments.
social_text: Following the U.S.-Iran ceasefire on April 7, investors poured $28B into U.S. stocks, reversing a $56B outflow. The S&P 500 is now 2% above pre-war levels, driven by strong earnings and economic resilience.

title: The million dollar retirement planning question: How long will you live?
summary:
Retirement planning poses a significant challenge for individuals, balancing the enjoyment of present moments with the necessity of preparing for an uncertain future. Wade Pfau, a professor at the American College of Financial Services, emphasizes the importance of pacing asset withdrawals to ensure that retirees do not outlive their savings. He advocates for a strategic approach to spending, suggesting that retirees should prioritize high-value experiences, such as travel or family time, while also being mindful of their financial longevity. Pfau's insights are particularly relevant as he discusses the need for a balanced investment strategy that evolves from aggressive asset growth pre-retirement to a more conservative approach post-retirement.

In his conversation with Yahoo Finance, Pfau elaborates on the roles of stocks and bonds in retirement portfolios. He explains that before retirement, individuals should focus on growing their assets through a diversified mix of stocks and bonds, with a greater emphasis on stocks to capitalize on market growth. However, once retired, the strategy shifts; bonds become essential for providing fixed income to cover immediate expenses, while stocks are reserved for long-term growth. This nuanced understanding of asset allocation is crucial for retirees to navigate market volatility effectively.

Additionally, Pfau touches on the emerging trend of target-date retirement funds incorporating annuity features, which could offer retirees more stability and predictability in their income streams. As the landscape of retirement planning continues to evolve, understanding these dynamics is vital for individuals seeking to secure their financial futures while enjoying their retirement years. Pfau's insights serve as a guide for retirees to make informed decisions that balance enjoyment with financial prudence.
social_text: Wade Pfau emphasizes the importance of pacing asset withdrawals in retirement to avoid outliving savings. He advocates for prioritizing experiences while balancing stocks for growth and bonds for fixed income. Strategic planning is key.

title: Tesla Will Report Q1 Earnings on April 22 — Bulls and Bears Clash Over TSLA Stock Targets
summary:
Tesla is set to announce its Q1 2026 earnings on April 22, a highly anticipated event that has generated significant debate among investors. The stock has recently experienced a 14.5% increase, surpassing the $400 mark, but remains down over 20% from its December 2025 peak. Analysts are divided on the stock's future, with price targets ranging dramatically from $25 to $600. This divergence reflects differing views on whether Tesla is merely a car manufacturer facing challenges or a forward-thinking company poised for growth through AI and robotics.

The upcoming earnings report is expected to reveal earnings per share (EPS) of $0.37, indicating a 37% year-over-year growth, while revenue is projected to reach $22.26 billion, a 15% increase from the previous year. However, the company has faced headwinds, including slower delivery growth and margin pressures due to price cuts and rising competition. In Q1 2026, Tesla delivered 358,023 vehicles, falling short of the anticipated 372,000 deliveries, which has raised concerns among investors.

As the earnings date approaches, the divide between bullish and bearish perspectives on Tesla intensifies. Bulls, led by analysts like Dan Ives from Wedbush, emphasize the company's AI initiatives and potential robotaxi rollout as key growth drivers. Conversely, bears express skepticism about Tesla's current EV business performance. The Q1 earnings report may not resolve these conflicting views but will likely provide insights into investor confidence and the company's strategic direction moving forward.
social_text: Tesla's Q1 2026 earnings report on April 22 is crucial, with EPS expected at $0.37 and revenue at $22.26B. Analysts' price targets range from $25 to $600, reflecting a split on Tesla's future as an AI-driven growth story or struggling car manufacturer.

title: COVID-era homeowners are still hanging on to their ultra-low-rate mortgages
summary:
The persistence of ultra-low mortgage rates from the COVID era continues to shape the housing market significantly. As of the end of last year, over half of all outstanding mortgages still had rates below 4%, a trend that reflects the reluctance of homeowners to sell or refinance their properties. This phenomenon, known as the 'lock-in effect,' has resulted in three consecutive years of home sales remaining near multi-decade lows. Homeowners are hesitant to relinquish their favorable mortgage rates, especially as current rates hover around 6.4%, which has led to a scarcity of available homes for prospective buyers.

The impact of the lock-in effect is profound, as it limits inventory and drives up home prices. The share of homeowners with sub-4% rates peaked at 65% in 2022, but has only gradually decreased due to the reluctance of locked-in homeowners and the introduction of interest rate buy-downs for new construction. This scarcity of sellers has created a challenging environment for buyers, who face limited choices and increased competition for available properties.

As mortgage rates rise, the financial implications for new buyers become stark. For instance, a buyer purchasing a $400,000 home with a 20% down payment would see their monthly payment jump from $1,528 at a 4% rate to $1,919 at 6%. Last year marked a significant milestone as the average monthly payment for homeowners surpassed $2,000 for the first time, highlighting the financial strain on new entrants in the market. With nearly 22% of mortgages now exceeding 6%, the housing market remains in a state of flux, awaiting a potential drop in rates to stimulate seller activity.
social_text: Over 50% of mortgages still below 4% as homeowners cling to low rates, causing a lock-in effect that limits inventory and drives prices up. Average monthly payments exceeded $2,000 for the first time last year.

title: Workers who want AI training the most are using it the least
summary:
A recent analysis from the New York Federal Reserve highlights a significant disparity in the adoption of generative AI among workers based on income and education levels. The study reveals that while AI adoption is highest among college graduates and high earners, lower-income workers are lagging behind. Specifically, only 15.9% of workers earning less than $50,000 utilize AI tools, compared to 66.3% of those earning over $200,000. This disparity raises concerns about potential exacerbation of labor market inequities, especially as 62% of workers fear that AI will lead to increased unemployment rates. Interestingly, even among high earners, there is a strong belief that AI will negatively impact job security, with 69.4% expressing concern about job losses due to AI advancements.

Despite the growing interest in AI, many workers, particularly younger individuals and those without college degrees, are eager for training opportunities. The New York Fed's findings indicate that only 16% of employed respondents have access to AI training from their employers, despite 38% considering such training essential for their job performance. This gap in training availability highlights a critical need for companies to invest in upskilling their workforce, particularly for those in lower-income brackets who are willing to pay for such opportunities. The analysis suggests that addressing these training needs could help mitigate fears surrounding job displacement and enhance overall productivity in the workforce.

The implications of these findings are significant for businesses and policymakers alike. As AI continues to evolve, understanding the dynamics of its adoption across different income levels will be crucial in shaping strategies that promote equitable access to technology and training. Companies that prioritize AI training for all employees, regardless of their income level, may not only improve job satisfaction but also foster a more inclusive work environment. This approach could ultimately lead to a more balanced labor market, where the benefits of AI are shared more broadly across all segments of the workforce.
social_text: A New York Fed analysis reveals stark AI adoption disparities: only 15.9% of workers earning < $50K use AI vs. 66.3% of those earning > $200K. 62% fear job losses due to AI, highlighting urgent training needs for lower-income workers.

title: Google Photos adds new touch-up tools for ‘quick’ fixes
summary:
Google Photos has introduced new touch-up tools aimed at enhancing user experience by allowing for quick and subtle edits directly within the app. Announced on Monday, these tools include options for removing blemishes, refining skin texture, brightening eyes, and whitening teeth, among others. Users can access these features by selecting a face in their photos and choosing from a variety of tools such as heal, smooth, under eyes, irises, teeth, eyebrows, or lips. Each tool offers adjustable intensity, giving users control over the final look of their images.

The rollout of these touch-up tools is gradual and is currently available for Android devices that meet specific requirements, including at least 4 GB of RAM and running Android 9.0 or higher. This move by Google is strategic, as it aims to keep users engaged within the Google Photos ecosystem, reducing the need to switch to third-party applications for photo editing. By providing these features, Google is enhancing the functionality of its photo management service, making it more appealing to users who seek convenience in their editing processes.

However, it is important to consider the psychological implications of photo retouching. Research indicates that frequent photo editing can lead to negative emotions, low self-esteem, and body image issues among users. As Google rolls out these tools, it may also want to promote responsible usage to mitigate potential adverse effects on mental health, ensuring that users enjoy the benefits of editing without compromising their self-image.
social_text: Google Photos now offers touch-up tools for quick edits, including blemish removal and teeth whitening. Available on Android devices with 4GB RAM and Android 9.0+, this feature aims to keep users editing within the app.

title: The 12-month window
summary:
In a recent episode of the podcast 'No Priors,' co-host Elad Gil discussed the critical importance of timing for business exits, particularly in the current climate of rapid deal-making. He emphasized that most companies experience a peak value period of about 12 months before their worth declines significantly. This insight is particularly relevant for founders who may be tempted to hold out for even better valuations, as history shows that successful exits often occur when companies recognize and act upon their peak moments. Gil cited examples like Lotus, AOL, and Broadcast.com, which all capitalized on their high points and achieved generational returns by selling at the right time.

To help founders navigate this crucial period, Gil recommended scheduling regular board meetings focused on exit strategies. By making these discussions a routine part of the agenda, companies can approach the topic with less emotional weight and more strategic clarity. This proactive approach can help teams evaluate their market position and assess whether they are nearing their peak value, allowing them to make informed decisions about potential exits.

As the landscape for AI startups evolves, the urgency of recognizing these peak moments becomes even more pronounced. With foundational models rapidly expanding, the window for maximizing company value may be narrowing. Founders, like Deel's CEO Alex Bouaziz, are already acknowledging the shifting dynamics in the industry, highlighting the need for vigilance and strategic planning in order to seize the right moment for an exit before the market changes again.
social_text: Elad Gil highlights a critical 12-month peak value window for companies, urging founders to schedule regular board meetings to discuss exit strategies. Recognizing this moment can lead to generational returns, as seen with Lotus and AOL.

title: Rolls-Royce Nightingale is a $5 million limited-edition EV, and it's already sold out
summary:
Rolls-Royce has unveiled the Nightingale, a limited-edition all-electric vehicle that marks the debut of its Coachbuild Collection. Priced between $4 million and $5 million, the Nightingale combines the elegance of classic luxury cars with modern engineering. This vehicle is designed to fill a niche between Rolls-Royce's standard offerings and bespoke commissions, such as the ultra-exclusive Boat Tail, which sold for $28 million. CEO Chris Brownridge emphasizes that the Nightingale is a collector's item, crafted through a collaborative process with clients who visit the Rolls-Royce Private Office to customize their vehicles.

The Nightingale is not just a car; it represents a lifestyle choice for the affluent, offering a unique blend of nostalgia and innovation. With only 100 units available globally, the exclusivity of the Nightingale is a significant selling point. Clients are invited to partake in a personalized experience at the Private Office, where they can select from a range of luxury materials and finishes, ensuring that each vehicle is tailored to their specific tastes and preferences.

This launch signifies Rolls-Royce's commitment to embracing electric mobility while maintaining its heritage of luxury and craftsmanship. The Nightingale is poised to attract high-net-worth individuals looking for a statement piece that reflects both their status and their values in sustainability. As the automotive industry shifts towards electrification, Rolls-Royce's foray into this market with such a high-end offering could redefine luxury electric vehicles and set new standards for exclusivity in the sector.
social_text: Rolls-Royce's Nightingale, priced at $4-5 million, is the first in the exclusive Coachbuild Collection, limited to 100 units. Clients customize their vehicles at the Private Office, blending luxury with electric innovation.

title: What the Allbirds 'Hail Mary' says about the AI trade right now
summary:
Allbirds, the sustainable sneaker brand, has made a surprising pivot towards artificial intelligence, rebranding itself as 'NewBirdAI' in a bid to capitalize on the current AI hype. The announcement came mid-week, resulting in a staggering nearly 600% surge in its stock price, which jumped from around $3 to over $10 per share. This drastic increase highlights the intense market interest in AI-related ventures, as companies scramble to align themselves with the latest technological trends. However, the specifics of Allbirds' transformation into an AI company remain vague, raising questions about the feasibility of this ambitious shift.

The company's strategy involves acquiring high-performance AI compute hardware and leasing it to customers who are underserved by existing hyperscalers or spot markets. Despite the excitement surrounding this announcement, experts express skepticism regarding Allbirds' ability to execute this pivot effectively. The leadership team primarily consists of individuals with backgrounds in apparel, lacking the necessary expertise in AI technology. This raises concerns about the company's capacity to navigate the complexities of the AI landscape, especially given the capital-intensive nature of building data centers and acquiring advanced technology.

Critics have labeled this move a 'Hail Mary' attempt to boost stock prices amid the AI frenzy. While some view it as a strategic play to attract investor interest, others doubt the company's long-term viability in the AI sector. With plans to raise $50 million, Allbirds faces significant challenges in competing against major tech players who have committed billions to AI infrastructure. As the company embarks on this uncertain journey, stakeholders will be closely monitoring its next steps and the potential impact on its market position.
social_text: Allbirds pivots to AI, rebranding as NewBirdAI, with plans to raise $50M for high-performance compute hardware. Stock surged nearly 600%, but skepticism remains about execution and expertise in the AI sector.

title: 'The results shocked me': A shopper compared 32 items at Walmart and Costco — and one store was nearly 26% cheaper
summary:
A recent comparison of grocery prices between Walmart and Costco revealed significant savings for consumers, with Costco's average prices being 21% lower than Walmart's. The analysis conducted by Business Insider's Savannah Born involved a detailed examination of 32 common grocery items, highlighting the price differences based on unit costs. While Walmart offered lower prices on certain items like chicken, sugar, and flour, Costco emerged as the more economical choice overall, particularly for bulk purchases. This finding is crucial for consumers looking to maximize their grocery budgets, especially in a landscape where food prices are rising and many Americans face challenges accessing affordable groceries.

The study also sheds light on the broader issue of food accessibility in the United States, where approximately 19 million people live in food deserts. For these individuals, the cost of traveling to grocery stores can negate any potential savings from shopping at discount retailers. The analysis emphasizes the importance of considering not just the price of items but also the associated costs of shopping, such as transportation and time, which can significantly impact overall savings.

As consumers navigate their grocery shopping options, understanding the value proposition of membership-based retailers like Costco becomes essential. With an annual membership fee of $65, shoppers need to frequent the store enough to offset this cost. The findings from this comparison serve as a valuable resource for budget-conscious consumers, providing insights into where they can find the best deals and how to make informed decisions about their grocery purchases.
social_text: A shopper compared 32 items at Walmart and Costco, finding Costco's prices 21% lower on average. While Walmart had cheaper chicken and flour, Costco was the overall better value, crucial for budget-conscious consumers.

title: ‘This Is Highway Robbery’: George Kamel Blasts Family Member’s Investment Fees
summary:
In a recent discussion, George Kamel from The Ramsey Show criticized the exorbitant fees proposed by a family member managing investments. The case revolves around a man named Dan, who entrusted his uncle-in-law with $10,000 two and a half years ago. The investment strategy, based on the S&P 500, has yielded a growth to $15,000. However, the uncle-in-law's proposal to formalize the arrangement includes a '2 and 20' fee structure, which entails a 2% asset under management (AUM) fee plus 20% of any profits exceeding a 10% threshold. Kamel argues that this fee structure is not only excessive but also atypical for individual investors, as it mirrors hedge fund compensation models that are designed for large-scale operations with extensive resources and expertise.

Kamel emphasizes the importance of understanding the implications of such fee structures, especially for individuals who may lack confidence in their financial knowledge and turn to family members for investment management. Registered investment advisors (RIAs) are legally bound to act in their clients' best interests, unlike unlicensed DIY investors who operate in a legal gray area. This situation highlights the potential pitfalls of mixing family relationships with financial investments, as it can lead to conflicts of interest and misunderstandings about the true cost of investment management.

The stark contrast in potential earnings under different fee structures illustrates the financial impact of these decisions. For example, if Dan's account grows to $24,000 over five years at a 10% annual growth rate, a standard 1% AUM fee would result in earnings of around $900 for the advisor, while the uncle's proposed structure could yield significantly higher fees, depending on the performance. This case serves as a cautionary tale for investors to carefully evaluate the fees and structures proposed by anyone managing their money, particularly family members, to ensure they are not overpaying for services that may not meet professional standards.
social_text: George Kamel critiques a family member's proposed 2-and-20 fee structure for managing investments, calling it 'highway robbery.' Standard fees are around 1%, highlighting the risks of family investment arrangements.

title: The Fed quietly altered its March inflation forecast — and it points to more pain for Americans. How to fight back
summary:
The recent adjustments to the Federal Reserve's inflation forecast indicate a troubling trend for American consumers, as inflation is projected to rise more rapidly than previously anticipated. The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics reported a 3.3% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) for the 12-month period ending in March 2026. This uptick is largely attributed to ongoing geopolitical tensions, particularly the military actions involving U.S. and Israeli forces against Iran, which have severely impacted global oil supply. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical transit route for oil, has exacerbated the situation, leading to a surge in gasoline prices, which have now exceeded $4 per gallon. The ripple effects of these fuel costs extend to transportation and shipping, ultimately affecting the prices of goods at grocery stores and other retail outlets.

In addition to rising fuel prices, the conflict has also disrupted agricultural operations in the U.S., coinciding with the sowing season and causing a staggering 30% increase in global fertilizer prices. This combination of factors suggests that consumers may face prolonged financial strain, as the costs of essential goods continue to rise. The Fed's revised outlook reflects a more pessimistic view of the economic landscape, indicating that inflationary pressures are likely to persist. As a result, American households may need to adjust their budgets and spending habits to cope with the anticipated increases in living costs.

To mitigate the impact of these economic challenges, individuals are encouraged to explore various strategies, such as investing in tax-efficient assets or seeking financial advice to navigate the complexities of inflation. With the potential for continued price hikes, it is crucial for consumers to stay informed and proactive in managing their finances. The current economic climate serves as a reminder of the interconnectedness of global events and their direct effects on everyday life in the U.S.
social_text: The Fed's inflation forecast has worsened, predicting a 3.3% rise in CPI by March 2026. Gas prices exceed $4/gallon, driven by geopolitical tensions and a 30% spike in fertilizer costs, impacting food prices.

title: Dear Seagate Technology Stock Fans, Mark Your Calendars for April 28
summary:
Seagate Technology Holdings plc (STX) is poised for a significant event on April 28, when it will report its fiscal Q3 2026 earnings. The company has experienced a remarkable surge in its stock price, reaching a 52-week high of $534.23, driven by increasing demand for high-capacity hard disk drives (HDDs) in artificial intelligence (AI) data centers and cloud infrastructure. Analysts have noted that the demand for HDDs is being fueled by hyperscalers who require massive storage capabilities to train large language models (LLMs). This trend has positioned Seagate favorably in the market, as supply constraints for components like memory ICs and optical parts continue to persist, further enhancing the demand for HDDs.

The optimism surrounding Seagate is echoed by major financial institutions. Citigroup has highlighted the solid demand linked to AI growth, while Morgan Stanley has pointed out that the demand for HDDs is expected to rise, with supply constraints potentially lasting until 2028. title: Cash has been a better portfolio diversifier than Treasurys, Morningstar finds. Where to nab attractive yields
summary:
According to a recent report by Morningstar, cash has emerged as a superior portfolio diversifier compared to Treasurys, particularly in the current economic climate. With cash-equivalent investments, such as money market funds, amassing a staggering $7.64 trillion in assets, investors are increasingly drawn to the attractive yields these instruments offer. Despite the Federal Reserve's rate cuts late last year, the annualized seven-day yield on the Crane 100 list of the largest taxable money funds remains robust at 3.45%. This yield allows investors to de-risk their portfolios while still achieving reasonable returns in the short term.

Amy Arnott, a portfolio strategist at Morningstar, emphasizes that cash provides diversification benefits that accumulate gradually over time, particularly during periods of interest rate fluctuations. Unlike investment-grade bonds, including Treasurys, cash has demonstrated a lower correlation with stocks over the past three years. This characteristic makes cash a more effective diversifier, especially as the correlation between stocks and bonds turned positive when the Fed began raising rates in 2022. As a result, bonds have not served as the reliable ballast that investors anticipated.

While the market is currently not pricing in further rate cuts this year, the steady yields on cash investments present a unique opportunity for investors to reassess their portfolio strategies. As correlations between stocks and high-quality bonds continue to decline, cash remains a critical component for those looking to enhance diversification and mitigate risk in their investment portfolios. The ongoing analysis of cash's role in portfolio management underscores its importance in navigating the complexities of the current financial landscape.

title: It’s not just one thing — it’s another thing
summary:

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn put a customer satellite in the wrong orbit during its third launch
summary:
Blue Origin's recent launch of its New Glenn rocket marked a significant milestone as it was the first time the company successfully reused a rocket. However, the mission was marred by a critical failure when the upper stage of the rocket placed the BlueBird 7 satellite into an orbit that was lower than intended. Although the satellite was able to separate from the rocket and power on, the altitude was insufficient for operational capabilities, necessitating its de-orbiting. This incident highlights the challenges Blue Origin faces as it seeks to establish itself in the competitive space launch market.

CEO Dave Limp acknowledged the failure, attributing it to one of the upper stage engines not producing enough thrust to reach the target orbit. While the satellite's loss is covered by insurance, this incident represents a significant setback for Blue Origin's New Glenn program, which has only recently begun operations after years of development. The company had previously launched twin spacecraft for NASA, making this failure particularly impactful as it seeks to expand its commercial offerings.

The implications of this failure extend beyond Blue Origin's immediate commercial interests. The Federal Aviation Administration has mandated an investigation into the incident, which could affect the company's future launch schedules and its ambitions to become a key player in NASA's Artemis missions. With a goal of launching 45 more satellites by the end of 2026, AST SpaceMobile remains optimistic, but the pressure is on Blue Origin to rectify the issues with its New Glenn rocket to maintain its competitive edge in the space industry.
social_text: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket failed to deliver the BlueBird 7 satellite to the correct orbit, necessitating de-orbiting. CEO Dave Limp cited insufficient thrust from the upper stage engine. The FAA has ordered an investigation.

title: Robots beat human records at Beijing half-marathon
summary:
In a remarkable display of technological advancement, a humanoid robot has set a new record at the Beijing half-marathon, finishing the race in an impressive 50 minutes and 26 seconds. This achievement not only surpasses the previous human world record of 57 minutes set by Jacob Kiplimo but also marks a significant improvement from last year's robot performance, where the fastest robot completed the race in two hours and 40 minutes. The event has sparked discussions about the fairness of comparing human and robot capabilities, with some commentators humorously noting that cars can also outrun cheetahs, highlighting the unique nature of robotic competition.

The winning robot, developed by Chinese smartphone manufacturer Honor, showcased the potential of autonomous technology in competitive environments. Interestingly, while the 50:26 robot was declared the winner based on a weighted scoring system, another Honor robot achieved a faster time of 48 minutes and 19 seconds but was remote-controlled. This distinction emphasizes the ongoing debate about the capabilities of autonomous versus remote-controlled robots in athletic competitions.

The event featured a mix of robot participants, with approximately 40% competing autonomously and 60% being remote-controlled. Despite the impressive performances, not all robots fared well, as evidenced by incidents where some robots fell at the starting line or collided with barriers. This half-marathon serves as a testament to the rapid advancements in robotics and artificial intelligence, raising questions about the future of human-robot competition in sports and other fields.

title: Dave Ramsey shares the ‘biggest 2 elements’ that will make you a millionaire, but is he right? Here’s the math
summary:

However, the current economic landscape poses significant challenges, particularly with the staggering levels of student debt and household debt in the U.S. As of the fourth quarter of 2025, American household debt reached an alarming $18.8 trillion, highlighting the financial burdens many face. This debt crisis is compounded by the fact that many graduates, especially those with professional degrees, struggle to pay off their loans, often taking decades to achieve financial stability.

The average American student loan borrower takes approximately 20 years to repay their debt, which can severely hinder their ability to save for retirement or invest in assets. Additionally, a study from the Schwartz Center for Economic Policy Analysis revealed that only 35% of Americans nearing retirement feel financially secure, indicating a widespread lack of confidence in achieving financial goals. The elder poverty rate in the U.S. stands at around 23%, significantly higher than in Canada, further underscoring the financial challenges faced by older Americans.

Despite these obstacles, Ramsey remains optimistic, encouraging individuals to take proactive steps towards financial health. The recent drop in mortgage rates to 5.99% offers a glimmer of hope for potential homebuyers, although the median home price remains high at $436,705. As Americans navigate these economic hurdles, Ramsey's advice serves as a reminder that with discipline and strategic planning, achieving millionaire status is still within reach, even in a challenging financial environment.

title: Borderlands Mexico: Truck exports to U.S. fall in March
summary:
In March, Mexico's heavy-duty truck sector experienced a decline in production and exports, reflecting ongoing challenges in the market. According to data from INEGI, Mexico produced 12,617 heavy-duty trucks in March 2026, marking a 6.6% decrease compared to the same month in 2025. Exports also fell, totaling 10,625 units, which is a 5.9% year-over-year decline. Industry experts attribute this downturn to a combination of structural and cyclical factors, including weak freight demand, cautious investment from fleets, and high inventory levels among North American carriers. Additionally, competition from used truck imports into Mexico has further complicated the landscape for domestic manufacturers.

Guillermo Rosales, president of the Mexican Association of Automotive Distributors (AMDA), highlighted the ongoing contraction in production, exports, and domestic sales, indicating that the industry is still recovering from a significant downturn that began in 2025. However, he noted that recent policy measures aimed at fleet renewal and restrictions on used truck imports could potentially bolster production and export demand in the latter part of 2026. Despite the current challenges, there are signs of a month-over-month rebound in both production and exports, suggesting a possible stabilization after a weak start to the year.

The U.S. remains the dominant market for Mexico's heavy-duty truck exports, accounting for an impressive 92% of shipments in the first quarter of 2026, which translates to 21,661 units. This reliance on the U.S. market underscores the importance of cross-border trade dynamics and the need for Mexican manufacturers to adapt to changing market conditions. As the industry navigates these challenges, the focus will be on leveraging policy support and improving competitiveness to enhance export performance moving forward.
social_text: In March 2026, Mexico's heavy-duty truck production fell 6.6% to 12,617 units, with exports down 5.9% to 10,625 units. The U.S. remains crucial, accounting for 92% of shipments, highlighting ongoing market challenges.

title: Advisors Tackle Decumulation as Americans Boom Into Retirement
summary:
As more than 4 million Americans reach the age of 65 each year, the challenge of decumulation becomes increasingly critical for retirees. Financial advisors emphasize the importance of strategic withdrawal planning to maximize retirement income while minimizing tax implications and Medicare premium impacts. This involves understanding clients' cash flow needs and the timing of withdrawals, which can significantly affect their financial stability in retirement. 

Advisors like Brian Eder from OnePoint BFG Wealth Partners advocate for the use of bucket strategies, which allocate investments based on different time horizons. This approach allows retirees to have immediate access to funds for living expenses while also investing for long-term growth. The strategy is designed to mitigate risks associated with market fluctuations, ensuring that retirees can weather economic downturns without jeopardizing their financial security.

A key concept in this planning is the sequence-of-return risk, highlighted by Chuck Failla of Sovereign Financial Group. This risk, often overlooked, can have a profound impact on a retiree's portfolio if not managed properly. By employing a well-structured withdrawal strategy, retirees can navigate the complexities of decumulation, ensuring that their savings last throughout their retirement years while maintaining their desired lifestyle and legacy goals.
social_text: With over 4 million Americans turning 65 each year, strategic decumulation is vital. Advisors recommend bucket strategies to manage withdrawals, mitigate sequence-of-return risk, and optimize tax implications for a secure retirement.

title: Tariff refunds begin on Monday. These retailers are due big paydays
summary:
U.S. importers, including major retailers like Target and Walmart, are set to receive over $160 billion in tariff refunds following a Supreme Court ruling that deemed certain tariffs imposed by the Trump administration as unlawful. The refund process is initiated with the launch of the Consolidated Administration and Processing of Entries (CAPE) portal by U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP), allowing importers to file claims for the tariffs they paid under the invalidated emergency tariff authority. This development has sparked optimism among retailers, who are eager to reclaim funds that could significantly impact their financial positions.

Despite the potential for substantial refunds, there is a prevailing sense of skepticism among companies and analysts regarding the efficiency of the claims process. Trade lawyers have raised concerns about bureaucratic obstacles and the possibility of legal challenges from the Trump administration, which could complicate or delay the refund process. Matthew Seligman, a trade attorney, expressed doubts about the government's willingness to facilitate a smooth refund experience, suggesting that importers should prepare for a challenging claims process.

As the CAPE portal opens, importers are encouraged to utilize the system to submit their claims, with the expectation of receiving a consolidated refund amount. However, analysts caution that the actual disbursement of funds may not occur as quickly as hoped. The situation remains fluid, and while the potential financial relief is significant, the complexities of the refund process could dampen the immediate benefits for retailers and other affected importers.

As Levine reflects on his experience, he emphasizes the importance of vigilance and skepticism in online interactions, especially when financial investments are involved. title: GE Aerospace (GE) and Indian Airforce Partner to Advance Aerospace Ecosystem
summary:

The depot facility will be owned and operated by the Indian Air Force, with GE Aerospace providing essential training support, spare parts, and specialized equipment. This initiative not only aims to enhance operational efficiency for the IAF but also underscores GE's commitment to fostering India's aerospace sector through local development and manufacturing. This partnership reflects a broader trend of increasing self-reliance in defense capabilities within India, aligning with the government's push for indigenous manufacturing. The establishment of this facility marks a pivotal step in strengthening the operational readiness of the Indian Air Force while enhancing GE's footprint in the region.

title: ‘Like the 1930s’: Ray Dalio warns Trump’s agenda could plunge the US into times ‘worse than a recession.’ How to prepare
summary:
Ray Dalio, the renowned investor and founder of Bridgewater Associates, has issued a stark warning regarding the potential economic consequences of former President Trump's tariff policies. He likens the current economic climate to the tumultuous 1930s, suggesting that the chaotic implementation of tariffs could lead to a breakdown of the established global monetary order. Dalio emphasizes that the manner in which tariffs are applied—whether in a stable or disruptive way—can significantly impact the economy. He argues that the current trajectory, marked by high levels of debt and geopolitical tensions, could result in profound changes that may not only lead to a recession but potentially something far worse for the average American. 

Dalio's concerns extend beyond mere economic downturns; he warns that the U.S. could face isolation as its major trading partners forge cross-border agreements that exclude the world's largest economy. This shift could destabilize the U.S.-backed global monetary system, which has been a cornerstone of international trade and finance since the end of World War II. He believes that the cyclical nature of economic systems suggests that these changes could be measured and anticipated, yet he stresses that they do not have to occur if managed properly.

In his remarks, Dalio draws on historical precedents to illustrate the potential for economic upheaval. He points out that the current situation mirrors past events where tariffs and debt levels contributed to significant economic disruptions. His insights serve as a call to action for policymakers and investors alike, urging them to consider alternative strategies for managing debt and fostering international cooperation to avoid repeating the mistakes of history. As the global economic landscape evolves, the implications of these policies could resonate for years to come.
social_text: Ray Dalio warns that Trump's tariff policies could lead to economic chaos worse than a recession, potentially isolating the U.S. as trading partners form exclusive agreements, threatening the global monetary system.

title: North Korea hackers blamed for $290M crypto theft
summary:
In a significant cyber heist, hackers have stolen over $290 million in cryptocurrency from Kelp DAO, a decentralized finance protocol that allows users to earn yields on idle crypto investments. This incident marks the largest crypto theft of 2023, surpassing the previous record set by a $285 million hack at the crypto exchange Drift in April. The attack was executed through the LayerZero bridge, which facilitates communication between different blockchains, exploiting vulnerabilities in Kelp DAO's security protocols that failed to implement multiple transaction verifications. 

LayerZero has publicly accused North Korea of orchestrating the attack, citing preliminary indicators linking the hack to the notorious hacking group TraderTraitor, known for targeting cryptocurrency platforms. This accusation aligns with a broader pattern of cybercrime attributed to North Korean hackers, who have increasingly turned to cryptocurrency theft as a means of funding the regime. In recent years, these hackers have amassed significant wealth through such operations, with estimates suggesting they stole over $2 billion in crypto last year alone.

Kelp DAO has responded to the allegations by shifting some blame back to LayerZero, indicating a potential dispute over security responsibilities. The incident raises concerns about the security measures in place within decentralized finance protocols and highlights the ongoing threat posed by state-sponsored hacking groups. As the cryptocurrency landscape continues to evolve, the need for robust security frameworks becomes ever more critical to protect against such high-stakes cyber thefts.
social_text: Hackers stole over $290M from Kelp DAO, marking 2023's largest crypto theft. LayerZero blames North Korea's TraderTraitor group, which has stolen $6B since 2017. Security flaws in Kelp's protocol facilitated the breach.

title: Mastodon says its flagship server was hit by a DDoS attack
summary:
Mastodon, the decentralized social networking platform, experienced a significant distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attack on its flagship server, mastodon.social, on Monday morning. The attack rendered the site intermittently unusable, leading to error messages and a full-screen outage warning for users. The company promptly acknowledged the issue in a status update around 7 a.m. ET, indicating that they were investigating the cyberattack and its implications for user accessibility. By 9:05 a.m. ET, Mastodon reported that it had implemented countermeasures to mitigate the attack, restoring access to the site, although some instability was expected to persist as the attack continued.

This incident follows closely on the heels of a similar DDoS attack that affected Bluesky, another decentralized social network, which had been grappling with outages for several days. While Bluesky managed to stabilize its service after a lengthy attack, the ongoing nature of DDoS attacks poses a significant challenge for decentralized platforms like Mastodon. These attacks, which flood servers with excessive traffic, aim to disrupt service rather than steal data, highlighting the vulnerabilities inherent in such networks.

The rise in DDoS attacks has been alarming, with security firms like Cloudflare reporting unprecedented levels of attack traffic. Last year, they mitigated a DDoS attack that peaked at 29.7 terabits per second, showcasing the growing scale and sophistication of these cyber threats. As decentralized social networks gain popularity, the frequency and intensity of DDoS attacks are likely to increase, necessitating robust security measures to ensure user accessibility and platform stability.
social_text: Mastodon faced a DDoS attack on its flagship server, mastodon.social, causing intermittent outages. The site was restored by 9:05 a.m. ET after countermeasures were implemented, but instability may persist as the attack continues.

title: Palantir posts mini-manifesto denouncing inclusivity and ‘regressive’ cultures
summary:
Palantir Technologies has recently published a 22-point summary of CEO Alex Karp's book, 'The Technological Republic,' which outlines the company's ideological stance and its vision for the future of technology in society. The summary, co-authored by Karp and Nicholas Zamiska, emphasizes the belief that Silicon Valley has a moral obligation to contribute positively to the nation that enabled its success. This statement comes amidst growing scrutiny of Palantir's partnerships with government agencies, particularly its work with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), which has sparked significant debate regarding the ethical implications of surveillance technologies in the context of immigration enforcement.

The manifesto critiques various cultural attitudes within the tech industry, including a dismissal of grand narratives and a perceived lack of accountability for the societal impacts of technology. Palantir argues that the survival of a culture hinges on its ability to foster economic growth and security, suggesting that the current cultural climate may be detrimental to these goals. The company positions itself as a defender of Western values, which it claims are under threat, and calls for a reevaluation of the tech sector's role in addressing these challenges.

This ideological declaration has drawn mixed reactions, with some critics labeling it as corporate propaganda rather than a genuine philosophical treatise. The timing of the post is notable, as it coincides with heightened scrutiny from lawmakers regarding the ethical use of surveillance technologies. As Palantir continues to navigate its controversial position in the tech landscape, its manifesto raises important questions about the responsibilities of technology companies in shaping societal norms and policies.
social_text: Palantir's 22-point manifesto, co-authored by CEO Alex Karp, argues Silicon Valley owes a moral debt to the U.S. while critiquing cultural attitudes that undermine economic growth and security amid scrutiny over its ICE partnerships.

title: Warsh commits to monetary policy independence, says president expressing views on rates isn't a threat
summary:
Kevin Warsh, the nominee for Federal Reserve chair, is set to address the Senate Banking Committee, emphasizing the critical importance of monetary policy independence. In his prepared remarks, Warsh asserts that the Federal Reserve must operate free from political pressures to ensure that decisions regarding interest rates are made based on rigorous analysis and thoughtful deliberation. He acknowledges that while elected officials, including the president, may express their opinions on monetary policy, these views do not inherently threaten the independence of the central bank.

Warsh's comments come in the context of President Trump's vocal criticisms of current Fed Chair Jerome Powell, whom he has labeled as incompetent and slow in response to economic conditions. Trump's administration has even initiated a criminal inquiry into Powell's conduct, which Warsh suggests is an attempt to exert pressure on the Fed to lower interest rates. Despite these challenges, Warsh maintains that the operational independence of the Fed is crucial for effective monetary policy and that central bankers must be resilient in the face of external opinions.

With a background as a former Fed governor and experience in economic policy at the White House, Warsh brings a wealth of knowledge to the role. He believes that while it is important for central bankers to consider a range of perspectives, the ultimate responsibility for maintaining independence lies with the Fed itself. His upcoming testimony is anticipated to shed light on his vision for the future of U.S. monetary policy and the balance between political influence and central bank autonomy.
social_text: Kevin Warsh emphasizes the necessity of Fed independence in his Senate testimony, stating that presidential opinions on rates do not threaten this autonomy. He advocates for strong, analytically driven monetary policy amidst political pressures.

title: Workers aren't happy with their pay. But they're not looking to switch jobs, either.
summary:
The latest labor market survey from the New York Federal Reserve reveals a troubling trend among workers regarding their job satisfaction, particularly concerning wages and promotion opportunities. In March, the likelihood of employees switching jobs fell to 9.7%, the lowest since March 2021, indicating a growing reluctance to leave current positions. This hesitance is reflected in the quit rate, which stands at 1.9% of total employment, as many workers opt to stay put amid fears of an uncertain job market. This situation creates a power imbalance favoring employers, potentially stifling wage competition and leaving consumers vulnerable to rising prices that may outstrip wage growth.

The survey also highlights a significant decline in wage satisfaction, dropping from 55.6% in November to 52.3% in March, marking the lowest level since the Fed began tracking this data in 2014. Lower-income workers are particularly affected, with only 30.9% of those earning less than $60,000 expressing satisfaction with their pay. Additionally, overall satisfaction with promotion opportunities has plummeted to 41.2%, with women reporting even lower satisfaction levels at 35.2%. This dissatisfaction is compounded by a record high minimum salary expectation of nearly $85,000 for workers considering a job change, suggesting that many feel undercompensated for their skills and contributions.

Despite these challenges, the labor market appears to be stabilizing, as evidenced by a decrease in the unemployment rate from 4.6% in November to 4.3% in March. However, the data indicates that while fewer individuals are actively seeking new employment, the underlying issues of wage dissatisfaction and limited promotion prospects remain critical concerns. As workers grapple with these realities, the dynamics of the job market continue to evolve, posing challenges for both employees and employers alike.
social_text: The New York Fed's survey shows worker satisfaction with wages dropped to 52.3%, the lowest since 2014. Only 30.9% of lower-income workers feel satisfied, while the minimum salary needed for a job change hits nearly $85,000.

title: MP Materials vs. USA Rare Earth: What's the Better Long-Term Play?
summary:
In the competitive landscape of rare-earth element production, MP Materials and USA Rare Earth are emerging as key players in the U.S. market. MP Materials currently holds a significant advantage, operating the only large-scale rare earth mine in the country, the Mountain Pass mine in California. This operational mine not only allows MP to supply critical materials but also positions it strategically within the supply chain, especially as demand for high-performance magnets continues to rise in various high-tech applications. In contrast, USA Rare Earth, while possessing the rights to the promising Round Top site, faces substantial challenges ahead, including a projected timeline that may not see mining operations commence until 2028. This delay raises questions about its ability to compete effectively in the near term, especially given the complexities involved in establishing a new mining operation, as evidenced by the historical difficulties faced by Mountain Pass itself.

Moreover, MP Materials is not just focused on mining; it is also expanding its manufacturing capabilities with plans for a second magnet factory set to break ground in 2026. This diversification into manufacturing enhances its value proposition and positions the company to capture a larger share of the market as the demand for rare-earth magnets grows. The recent profitable quarter reported by MP Materials further solidifies its strong standing in the industry, showcasing its operational efficiency and market readiness. On the other hand, USA Rare Earth, despite having $3.1 billion in funding, lacks the operational experience necessary to navigate the complexities of mine development, which could hinder its progress and market entry.

In conclusion, while both companies have the potential to thrive in the U.S. rare-earth supply chain, MP Materials currently holds a dominant position due to its operational mine and manufacturing capabilities. The future of USA Rare Earth will depend heavily on its ability to overcome the inherent challenges of establishing a new mine and its capacity to execute its ambitious plans effectively. As the global demand for rare-earth elements continues to escalate, the competition between these two companies will be crucial in shaping the future of the U.S. supply chain for these critical materials.
social_text: MP Materials leads the U.S. rare-earth market with the only operational mine, Mountain Pass, and plans for a second magnet factory by 2026. USA Rare Earth, with $3.1B funding, faces challenges to open Round Top by 2028.

This shift is driven by considerations of total cost and safety, as OEMs leverage existing stamping equipment to maintain efficiency. The company has reported a full order book, with automotive steel bookings on the rise as manufacturers prioritize supply certainty over the vulnerabilities associated with aluminum supply chains. This trend is further supported by trade enforcement measures, which have significantly reduced U.S. steel imports to their lowest levels since 2009, creating a more predictable domestic market environment.

title: Three psychedelics stocks surge after Trump executive order. Oppenheimer sees more upside ahead

title: FAA orders investigation into Blue Origin’s New Glenn mishap
summary:
The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) has mandated an investigation into Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket following a failure of its upper stage during a recent launch. This incident occurred during the rocket's third flight, which initially showcased a significant achievement for Blue Origin: the successful reuse of a New Glenn booster that landed on a drone ship after launch. However, the mission's primary goal—to deliver a communications satellite for AST SpaceMobile—was compromised when the satellite was placed in a lower-than-expected orbit, rendering it a total loss. The FAA's directive means that Blue Origin must complete the investigation before it can proceed with any further New Glenn launches, which could hinder the company's plans for up to 12 additional missions this year.

The failure has significant implications for Blue Origin's competitive positioning in the space launch industry, particularly against SpaceX, which has established itself as a leader in rocket reusability. While Blue Origin has made strides in developing its technology, this setback raises questions about its reliability and operational readiness. The company has not publicly addressed the incident, leaving stakeholders and the market in uncertainty regarding the timeline for resolution and future launches.

AST SpaceMobile, the satellite's customer, has indicated that it will write off the lost satellite but has insurance to cover the financial impact. The company's stock experienced a notable decline following the news, reflecting investor concerns over the incident's ramifications. Despite this setback, AST SpaceMobile is optimistic about its future, with multiple new satellites nearing completion and contracts with other launch providers, ensuring that it can continue its operations and plans for satellite deployment in the near term.
social_text: FAA orders Blue Origin to investigate New Glenn rocket failure, impacting plans for 12 launches this year. AST SpaceMobile's satellite lost, stock down 10%. Insurance will cover the loss, new satellites ready soon.

title: NSA spies are reportedly using Anthropic’s Mythos, despite Pentagon feud
summary:
The National Security Agency (NSA) is reportedly utilizing Anthropic's Mythos Preview, a sophisticated AI model designed for cybersecurity tasks, despite ongoing tensions with the Department of Defense (DoD). This development follows the DoD's classification of Anthropic as a 'supply chain risk' after the company restricted access to its model's full capabilities. Anthropic had previously announced that Mythos would not be publicly released due to its potential for offensive cyber operations, limiting access to approximately 40 organizations, including the NSA and the UK’s AI Security Institute.

Mythos is intended for scanning environments to identify exploitable vulnerabilities, which aligns with the NSA's objectives in enhancing national cybersecurity measures. The situation highlights a complex relationship between Anthropic and the U.S. government, particularly as the Pentagon has been engaged in legal disputes over the use of AI tools for surveillance and military applications. Anthropic's refusal to provide unrestricted access to its models has raised concerns about national security, prompting the DoD to take a cautious stance.

Despite these tensions, there are signs of a thawing relationship between Anthropic and the current administration. Recently, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei met with key White House officials, including Chief of Staff Susie Wiles and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, with reports indicating that the meeting was productive. This evolving dynamic suggests that while the NSA is leveraging Anthropic's technology, the broader implications for AI governance and national security remain contentious and complex.
social_text: The NSA is using Anthropic's Mythos Preview for cybersecurity, despite the Pentagon's concerns over supply chain risks. Mythos is limited to 40 organizations, focusing on vulnerability scanning amid ongoing legal disputes over AI use in national security.

title: WhatsApp is testing a premium subscription, put it is mainly cosmetic
summary:

The initiative appears to be inspired by similar offerings from platforms like Instagram and Snapchat, which have successfully introduced paid tiers to enhance user engagement. According to Meta, the parent company of WhatsApp, this test is in its early stages, focusing on gathering user feedback to ensure the features are genuinely valuable to subscribers.

The premium subscription will enable users to pin up to 20 chats, a significant increase from the current limit of three for free users. Additionally, subscribers will have the ability to create custom lists and apply various themes and notification tones to their chat lists. However, it is important to note that the subscription does not include any functional enhancements, such as ad removal from the Status feature, which has been a point of contention among users since its introduction.

Pricing details for WhatsApp Plus have not been officially confirmed, but reports suggest a potential cost of €2.49 per month in Europe and approximately $0.82 in Pakistan. Users will also have the opportunity to try the service with a one-month free trial. This move marks a significant shift for WhatsApp, which previously charged a subscription fee before eliminating it in 2016 after being acquired by Facebook. The introduction of this premium tier reflects Meta's ongoing strategy to monetize its messaging platform, which has seen substantial revenue growth in recent years, particularly from paid messaging services.
social_text: WhatsApp is testing a premium subscription, WhatsApp Plus, offering cosmetic features like custom themes and ringtones. Users can pin 20 chats, with pricing around €2.49/month in Europe and $0.82 in Pakistan. A one-month free trial is available.

title: TechCrunch Mobility: Uber enters its assetmaxxing era
summary:
Uber is embarking on a significant shift in its business strategy, committing over $10 billion to the development and acquisition of autonomous vehicle technology. The company has made strategic partnerships and investments in various autonomous vehicle firms, including WeRide, Lucid, Nuro, Rivian, and Wayve, indicating a robust commitment to becoming a leader in the autonomous transportation sector.

Historically, Uber has oscillated between asset-light and asset-heavy strategies. After an ambitious phase from 2015 to 2018, where it invested heavily in projects like Uber Elevate and its in-house autonomous vehicle unit, Uber ATG, the company pivoted away from this model in 2020. It sold off several of its ambitious projects but retained equity stakes in them, suggesting a cautious approach to its investments in emerging technologies.

Now, as Uber re-enters an asset-heavy phase, it is focusing on acquiring existing technologies rather than developing them internally. This strategic pivot could position Uber to capitalize on the rapidly evolving autonomous vehicle market, potentially reshaping its business model and enhancing its competitive edge in the transportation industry. The implications of this investment strategy could be profound, not only for Uber's future but also for the broader landscape of mobility and transportation technology.
